levels.title: The Table
levels.subtitle: Understanding the Game
levels.description: Get oriented at the bridge table. Learn how a deal flows from shuffle to score, how tricks and trumps work, how the auction determines the contract, and how the four players take on their unique roles.

levels.title: The Hand
levels.subtitle: Evaluating What You Hold
levels.description: Before you can bid, you need to know what your hand is worth. Master high card points, distributional points, hand shape, and the key point ranges that drive every bidding decision.

levels.title: The Opening
levels.subtitle: Starting the Conversation
levels.description: The opening bid is your first message to partner. Learn when to open, which bid to choose — from one of a suit to 1NT, strong 2C, weak twos, and preempts — and why each opening tells a different story.

levels.title: The Response
levels.subtitle: Answering Your Partner
levels.description: Your partner opened — now it's your turn. Learn the right response to every opening bid type: raises, new suits, notrump bids, Stayman, Jacoby transfers, and more.

levels.title: The Rebid
levels.subtitle: Completing the Picture
levels.description: The rebid completes the story your opening bid started. Learn how opener and responder refine their messages to land in the best contract, from partscore to slam.

levels.title: The Play
levels.subtitle: Declarer Technique
levels.description: Winning the auction is only half the battle. Master the declarer techniques that turn contracts into made contracts: counting winners and losers, finesses, entries, hold-up plays, and ruffing strategy.

levels.title: The Defense
levels.subtitle: Beating Their Contract
levels.description: Defense is the hardest part of bridge — you can't see partner's hand. Learn opening leads, third-hand and second-hand play, defensive signals, counting, and when to attack versus wait.

levels.title: The Conventions
levels.subtitle: Expanding Your Language
levels.description: Conventions are the vocabulary of competitive bridge. Add Blackwood, negative doubles, takeout doubles, overcalls, Michaels, and advanced NT and slam tools to your bidding arsenal.

levels.title: The Battle
levels.subtitle: Competitive Bidding
levels.description: When both sides are bidding, the rules change. Learn the Law of Total Tricks, balancing, sacrifice bidding, forcing passes, and the judgment calls that win competitive auctions.

levels.title: The Expert
levels.subtitle: Advanced Strategy
levels.description: Bridge mastery goes beyond technique. Explore scoring formats, hand reading, probability, safety plays, squeeze play, deception, and the mental game that separates experts from the rest.
